title: Remove a tote from the system using the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen
summary: Use the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen to scan a tote bar code, verify extract-side information received from the WCS, confirm the tote is properly positioned on the extract side, and press CONFIRM & EXECUTE to remove the tote from the system so the station operator can perform the required task.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when a tote must be removed from the system at the Hospital Station using the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen, after the tote is properly positioned on the extract side and the station operator needs to perform the required task.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to add a tote back into the system; the source only identifies the Add Tote screen as a shortcut from this screen.
Do not press CONFIRM & EXECUTE until the tote is positioned properly on the extract side.
Do not use this runbook as a recovery procedure for missing or unexpected Tote ID, Bar Code, or WCS Status information; the source does not provide recovery or escalation steps for those conditions.

responsibilities: Open or use the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen.
Scan the tote bar code with the green scanner.
Review extract position information shown from the WCS.
Verify the tote is properly positioned on the extract side.
Press CONFIRM & EXECUTE to remove the tote from the system.

safety_notes: Do not press CONFIRM & EXECUTE until the tote is positioned properly on the extract side.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen

access_required: Access to the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen
system_preconditions: The tote is properly positioned on the extract side before CONFIRM & EXECUTE is pressed.
Extract position information is available from the WCS on the Remove Tote screen.

visual_references.description: Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen showing Tote ID field, Barcode control, CONFIRM & EXECUTE button, extract position information, and Add Tote shortcut button.
Hospital Station operation artifact providing contextual tote-handling workflow at the hospital station.

steps.title: Open the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen
steps.purpose: Access the correct HMI screen used to remove a tote from the system.
steps.instruction: Open or use the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen.
steps.expected_result: The Remove Tote screen is displayed and available for tote processing.
steps.healthy_condition: The Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen is visible and shows the controls and extract position information area used for tote removal.
steps.failure_condition: The Remove Tote screen is not available or the operator is on a different screen.

actions.label: Open Remove Tote screen
actions.exact_action: Open or use the Hospital HMI "Remove Tote" screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Remove Tote screen cannot be accessed.

steps.title: Scan the tote bar code with the green scanner
steps.purpose: Populate the Tote ID using the tote bar code on the Remove Tote screen.
steps.instruction: Use the green scanner to scan the tote bar code so the Tote ID is populated.
steps.expected_result: The Tote ID is populated on the Remove Tote screen after the scan.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote bar code scans successfully and the Tote ID field populates.
steps.failure_condition: The Tote ID does not populate after scanning the tote bar code.

actions.label: Scan tote bar code
actions.exact_action: Use the green scanner to scan the tote bar code so the Tote ID is populated.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Tote ID field and barcode-related controls on the Remove Tote screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The Tote ID does not populate after scanning.

source_evidence.text: Use the green scanner to scan the tote bar code so the Tote ID is populated.
The nearby text states that the operator uses the green scanner to scan the tote bar code to populate the Tote ID and Barcode information.

Nearby source text states that when a tote arrives, an AGV brings it to the right side of the hospital station, the operator opens the corresponding HMI screen to extract the tote based on status fields, scans the tote barcode using the green scanner, confirms the Tote ID field auto-populates...

steps.title: Use the Barcode control if needed and rescan
steps.purpose: Use the screen barcode control when needed before scanning the tote bar code.
steps.instruction: If needed, press the Barcode control and then use the green scanner to scan the tote bar code.
steps.expected_result: The barcode scan is accepted through the Barcode control workflow and the tote information is populated.
steps.healthy_condition: After pressing the Barcode control and scanning, the tote information populates as expected.
steps.failure_condition: Pressing the Barcode control and rescanning does not populate the tote information.

actions.label: Rescan tote bar code
actions.exact_action: Use the green scanner to scan the tote bar code.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Barcode control and Tote ID/barcode fields on the Remove Tote screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote information still does not populate after using the Barcode control and rescanning.

source_evidence.text: If needed, press the Barcode control and then use the green scanner to scan the tote bar code.
The provided section_id and title reference "Add Tote But-", but the actual section_text is for "1.2 REMOVE TOTE" and contains actionable procedure content for tote removal.

steps.title: Check extract position information from the WCS
steps.purpose: Verify the tote and AGV information shown by the system before removal.
steps.instruction: Check the Extract Position Information shown from the WCS, including AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.
steps.expected_result: The operator can view extract-side information from the WCS for the tote being removed.
steps.healthy_condition: Extract Position Information is visible and includes AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.
steps.failure_condition: Extract Position Information is missing, incomplete, or does not appear as expected.

actions.label: Review WCS extract information
actions.exact_action: Check the Extract Position Information shown from the WCS, including AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Extract Position Information area showing AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.

stop_or_escalate_if: Extract Position Information does not appear as expected.

source_evidence.text: Check the Extract Position Information shown from the WCS, including AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.
The screen includes extract position information received from the WCS, including AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status...

steps.title: Verify the tote is properly positioned on the extract side
steps.purpose: Confirm the physical tote position is correct before executing removal.
steps.instruction: Verify the tote is positioned properly on the extract side.
steps.expected_result: The tote is confirmed to be properly positioned for removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote is properly positioned on the extract side.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not properly positioned on the extract side.

actions.label: Verify extract-side tote position
actions.exact_action: Verify the tote is positioned properly on the extract side.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote is not positioned properly on the extract side.

source_evidence.text: Verify the tote is positioned properly on the extract side.

The nearby text states that the operator uses the green scanner to scan the tote bar code to populate the Tote ID and Barcode information, then presses CONFIRM & EXECUTE after the tote is properly positioned on the extract side.

steps.title: Press CONFIRM & EXECUTE to remove the tote
steps.purpose: Execute tote removal in the system so the station operator can perform the required task.
steps.instruction: Press CONFIRM & EXECUTE to remove the tote from the system so the station operator can perform the required task.
steps.expected_result: The tote is removed from the system and the station operator can perform the required task.
steps.healthy_condition: The system accepts the CONFIRM & EXECUTE action and the tote is removed from the system.
steps.failure_condition: The tote is not removed from the system after CONFIRM & EXECUTE, or the operator cannot confirm successful removal from the available source evidence.

actions.label: Confirm tote removal
actions.exact_action: Press CONFIRM & EXECUTE to remove the tote from the system so the station operator can perform the required task.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote is not properly positioned on the extract side.
The Tote ID, Bar Code, or WCS Status information does not appear as expected before execution.

source_evidence.text: Press CONFIRM & EXECUTE to remove the tote from the system so the station operator can perform the required task.
Do not press CONFIRM & EXECUTE until the tote is positioned properly on the extract side.

The nearby text states that the operator uses the green scanner to scan the tote bar code to populate the Tote ID and Barcode information, then presses CONFIRM & EXECUTE after the tote is properly positioned on the extract side.

success_criteria: The tote is removed from the system.
The station operator can perform the required task.
The operator completed the action using the Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen after verifying extract-side information and tote position.
healthy_conditions: The Hospital HMI Remove Tote screen is available.
The Tote ID populates after scanning the tote bar code.
Extract Position Information from the WCS is visible and includes AGV#, Tote ID, Bar Code, and Status.
The tote is properly positioned on the extract side before execution.
CONFIRM & EXECUTE removes the tote from the system.
failure_conditions: The Remove Tote screen cannot be accessed.
The Tote ID does not populate after scanning the tote bar code.
Extract Position Information is missing, incomplete, or does not appear as expected.
The tote is not properly positioned on the extract side.
The source does not provide recovery or escalation steps if the Tote ID, Bar Code, or WCS Status information does not appear as expected.
escalation_guidance: Stop before pressing CONFIRM & EXECUTE if the tote is not positioned properly on the extract side.
If the Tote ID, Bar Code, or WCS Status information does not appear as expected, the source packet does not provide a recovery path; escalate for SME or supervisory review per local practice.
